input.headline: Thousands of US hazardous sites are at risk of flooding because of sea level rise, study finds

raw.headline: Thousands of US hazardous sites are at risk of flooding because of sea level rise, study finds

winner: Oh, good, another beautiful day to go to work at a Superfund site—nothing like standing knee-deep in mystery sludge while your boss casually mentions they're "evaluating relocation options.

candidates.text: Oh, good, another beautiful day to go to work at a Superfund site—nothing like standing knee-deep in mystery sludge while your boss casually mentions they're "evaluating relocation options.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on the headline with a relatable, exaggerated scenario that ties humor to the environmental issue."}

candidates.text: Why did the toxic waste dump start selling beachfront property? Because it knew sea levels were going up!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Puns on 'toxic waste dump' and 'beachfront property' are clever and specific, aligning well with the headline's topic. The joke is concise, surprising, and naturally phrased."}

candidates.text: What a great time to be a toxic waste site—you can finally take that beach vacation you always dreamed of!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on a toxic waste site 'taking a beach vacation' is both specific and funny, with natural English and good constraint compliance."}

candidates.text: Why did the Superfund site bring a ladder to the beach? It heard the water was rising—turns out, it just needed a little elevation!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a specific reference to 'Superfund site' which ties into the headline, and includes a surprise twist with the ladder. However, it's slightly generic in its humor and could be more tightly connected to the study's findings."}

candidates.text: Why did the toxic waste dump bring a ladder to work? It wanted to see how high the tides could *rise*!
